debate_rounds.title: Chair Initial Assessment
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 8 adds publish-then-validate integration tests and CLI smoke tests. The 3 publish-then-validate tests verify that cmd_publish output for nextjs/hugo/astro passes _validate_frontmatter, partially addressing the 'no end-to-end test verifying frontmatter renders in real SSG' gap. The 3 CLI smoke tests verify that the CLI binary works end-to-end (--help, draft command, validate command). Test coverage grew from 128 to 134 (+6). The CI fix (dynamic PROJECT_ROOT) demonstrates production-readiness. These are meaningful effectiveness improvements.

experts.rationale: Breadth: Publish-then-validate integration tests bridge the gap between publish and validation. CLI smoke tests verify end-to-end CLI functionality. Depth: The publish-then-validate tests verify actual output from cmd_publish passes SSG frontmatter validation. CLI smoke tests verify subprocess execution. Thickness: 134 tests including integration and CLI smoke tests. Effectiveness: The publish-then-validate tests catch SSG incompatibilities that individual unit tests miss. CLI smoke tests verify the tool works as a CLI, not just as library functions.

Breadth: Publish-then-validate and CLI smoke tests are new integration test categories. Depth: The publish-then-validate tests verify that frontmatter generated by cmd_publish passes validation for all 3 major SSG platforms. The CLI smoke tests verify actual subprocess execution. Thickness: 134 tests with good integration coverage. Effectiveness: These tests verify real-world usage patterns: publish output must be valid, CLI must work as a command-line tool.

Breadth: Publish-then-validate integration tests and CLI smoke tests add genuine breadth. The publish-then-validate tests verify a critical pipeline flow: generate frontmatter, validate it. Depth: The integration tests verify cmd_publish output passes _validate_frontmatter for nextjs/hugo/astro. The CLI smoke tests verify the CLI works as a subprocess. Thickness: 134 tests. The publish-then-validate and CLI smoke tests are high-value integration tests. Effectiveness: The publish-then-validate tests directly verify the SSG compatibility concern. The CLI smoke tests verify the tool works end-to-end.

Breadth: Integration tests for publish-then-validate and CLI smoke tests are meaningful additions. Depth: The publish-then-validate tests verify frontmatter passes validation for all platforms. The CLI smoke tests verify the tool works as a CLI. Thickness: 134 tests with good integration coverage. Effectiveness: The publish-then-validate tests bridge the gap between publishing and validation. The CLI smoke tests verify the tool works as a CLI, not just as imported functions.

Breadth: Integration tests and CLI smoke tests are real additions. Depth: The publish-then-validate tests verify format correctness, not rendering correctness. A frontmatter that passes validation can still fail to render. The CLI smoke tests are basic — they check that the CLI doesn't crash, not that it produces correct output. Thickness: 134 tests. Good number but the integration tests are format-level, not rendering-level. Effectiveness: The tests are necessary but not sufficient for SSG compatibility verification.

challenges.challenge: Lily scores effectiveness at 24, but the publish-then-validate tests verify format, not rendering. The CLI smoke tests verify the CLI runs, not that it produces correct results. These are necessary but not sufficient. I propose reducing from 24 to 23.

debate_rounds.title: Advocate Supports

supports.support: 134 tests including publish-then-validate integration tests for 3 SSG platforms and CLI smoke tests that verify actual subprocess execution. These are meaningful integration tests that verify real pipeline flows.

debate_rounds.statement: 134 tests with integration coverage across publishing, validation, and CLI execution is strong.

debate_rounds.title: Chair Synthesis
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 8 advances from 91 to 93. The publish-then-validate integration tests partially address the SSG rendering gap — they verify format correctness, which catches the most common SSG build failures. The CLI smoke tests verify the tool works as a CLI, which is a basic production-readiness check. Remaining gaps: (1) scoring model uses regex heuristics, (2) draft generates scaffolding not content, (3) no actual SSG build test. These are genuinely hard to close without external dependencies. The project is approaching convergence.

gaps: Scoring model uses regex heuristics, not validated against real search performance data
Draft generates scaffolding, not full article content (requires LLM integration)
No actual SSG build test (frontmatter validation checks format, not rendering in real SSG)
No semantic relevance scoring for internal link suggestions
No automated A/B testing or performance tracking for published articles
